items.title: Keep Your Trail Blazing With The UGREEN x Honkai: Star Rail Collaboration!
items.summary: miHoYo games are no stranger to the odd collaboration, be it with another title or even a car; however, UGREEN,...
items.content:
miHoYo games are no stranger to the odd collaboration, be it with another title or even a car; however, UGREEN, the consumer technology brand, seems to be ahead of the curve as their second collab is unveiled! Leaving the fantastical lands of Teyvat and entering the world of Honkai: Star Rail , the newest collab brings with it a cornucopia of on-the-go charging!

Titled "Power Up for the Next Trailblaze", the new range of products is themed around 5-star Elation character Yao Guan and consists of five main pieces of on-the-go gear: a 13.9mm ultra-thin magnetic power bank, a 65W fast charger, a 10,000mAh smart display fast-charging power bank, a 100W retractable fast-charging data cable, and an anti-loss tracker.

In addition to the aforementioned tech, the collaboration also provides some fun Yao Gaun-themed items, such as a chibi character stand, a transparent card, a badge, a holographic ticket, a lanyard, and a storage pouch!

The Honkai: Star Rail X UGREEN products are available now and will gradually roll out to online and offline retail channels worldwide.

GameGrin are proud to have all their articles researched, written, and edited by real people that care about gaming.

items.title: HoYoverse And UGREEN Launch Honkai: Star Rail Themed Charging Accessories
Ugreen x Honkai: Star Rail: Gaming Collaboration Unveiled
items.summary: Ugreen and Honkai: Star Rail are releasing five Yao Guang accessories plus a collector's box. Here's an overview of all the details on the power banks, charger, cable, and tracker.
items.content:
Share Link copied! (function() { window.afCopyLink = function(button, url) { if (navigator.clipboard && navigator.clipboard.writeText) { navigator.clipboard.writeText(url).then(function() { afShowSuccess(button); }).catch(function() { afFallbackCopy(url, button); }); } else { afFallbackCopy(url, button); } }; function afFallbackCopy(text, button) { var ta = document.createElement('textarea'); ta.value = text; ta.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;opacity:0'; document.body.appendChild(ta); ta.select(); try { document.execCommand('copy') && afShowSuccess(button); } catch (e) { alert('Bitte manuell kopieren: ' + text); } document.body.removeChild(ta); } function afShowSuccess(button) { var tooltip = button.querySelector('.copy-tooltip'); if (tooltip) { tooltip.classList.add('show'); } button.classList.add('copied'); setTimeout(function() { if (tooltip) { tooltip.classList.remove('show'); } button.classList.remove('copied'); }, 2000); } if (navigator.share) { var nativeBtn = document.querySelector('.af-share-native'); if (nativeBtn) { nativeBtn.style.display = 'flex'; nativeBtn.addEventListener('click', function(e) { e.preventDefault(); navigator.share({ title: document.title, url: window.location.href }); }); } } })(); Ugreen and HoYoverse are teaming up: The first officially licensed Ugreen x Honkai: Star Rail collection includes five charging accessories featuring Yao Guang designs, plus a limited-edition collector’s box. The series initially launched in the U.S., with prices ranging from $19.99 to $249.99. For fans, this means power banks, a GaN charger, a retractable cable, and a tracker—all styled after the Xianzhou General.

Following last year’s Genshin Impact collaboration, this marks Ugreen’s second foray into the HoYoverse universe. This time, the spotlight is on Yao Guang, a 5-star character introduced in Version 4.0. The series’ motto: “Power Up for the Next Trailblaze.”

For the designs, Ugreen has brought on board Yao Guang, the General of the Xianzhou Yuque. The designs draw inspiration from her role as an arbiter who interprets fate and turns misfortune into good fortune. But what matters isn’t just the aesthetics—it’s also the technology behind them.

This magnetic power bank offers 10,000 mAh and can charge up to three devices simultaneously, both wired and wirelessly. An integrated USB-C cable doubles as a carrying strap. Price in the U.S.: $89.99.

Image: Ugreen Nexode Pro Power Bank Also with 10,000 mAh, but featuring up to 45 W fast charging, an integrated USB-C cable, and a smart display that shows battery level and charging status. Price: $59.99. This is the only model not included in the collector’s box.

Image: Ugreen Nexode Air Charger The GaN charger delivers up to 65 W via a single USB-C port and, according to Ugreen, is about 46 percent more compact than standard 65-W power adapters. The foldable prongs make it ideal for travel. Price: $49.99.

Image: Ugreen Nexode Retractable USB-C Cable The retractable cable delivers up to 100 W, making it suitable for laptops as well. Data transfers at speeds of up to 480 Mbit/s. At $19.99, it’s the most affordable individual product in the series.

Image: Ugreen FineTrack Mini 2 The tracker works with Apple’s “Find My” network to locate lost items. A 600-mAh CR2450 battery is rated to last for up to 2,700 search operations, and a 110-dB signal helps you locate the item. Price: $21.99.

The limited-edition Collectible Gift Box includes four of the five devices: the MagFlow Air Power Bank, the Nexode Air Charger, the retractable USB-C cable, and the FineTrack Mini 2. It also includes exclusive Yao Guang merchandise: an acrylic display stand, a metal pin, a holographic ticket, and a pouch for the power bank. The box opens like a peacock’s tail, which fits the theme perfectly.

Anyone familiar with the Ugreen x Genshin Impact Edition already knows roughly what to expect: solid charging technology in a package aimed more at fans than at bargain hunters.

The catch for local Trailblazers: Ugreen has so far only announced the series for the U.S. There, it’s available for purchase on Amazon, the official Ugreen store, and the TikTok Shop. Additionally, Ugreen will be showcasing the products alongside Honkai: Star Rail at Anime Expo in Los Angeles from July 2–5, 2026, where they’ll be sold at the official merch booth.

Image: Ugreen A date for a German market launch has not yet been announced. However, with the Genshin collaboration, it didn’t take long for the accessories to appear here as well on de.ugreen.com and Amazon.de. A similar pattern is therefore conceivable, though it’s not guaranteed.

If you’re already looking for a portable battery and aren’t set on the fan-inspired design, it’s worth checking out our roundup of the best power banks . And to see how Ugreen’s standard magnetic power bank performs in everyday use, check out our review of the MagFlow Power Bank .

A German launch date has not yet been confirmed. Currently, the collection is only available in the U.S.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Collaborates With UGREEN And Launch Yao Guang Products
items.summary: Honkai: Star Rail and UGREEN are collaborating on a new line of products. This line heavily features Yao Guang.
items.content:
HoYoverse and the world-leading consumer electronics tech brand UGREEN have jointly launched a brand-new series of Honkai: Star Rail -themed charging and travel accessories. Themed “Power Up for the Next Trailblaze,” this collaboration centers around the highly popular game character Yao Guang, introducing five core pieces of on-the-go gear: a 13.9mm ultra-thin magnetic power bank, a 65W fast charger, a 10,000mAh smart display fast-charging power bank, a 100W retractable fast-charging data cable, and an anti-loss tracker that supports the Apple Find My ecosystem.

UGREEN is dedicated to creating tech products that deliver both exceptional quality and an outstanding experience, giving users worldwide effortless control over their digital lives. Meanwhile, Honkai: Star Rail crafts a cosmic adventure across the sea of stars. The Trailblazer embarks on an interstellar journey, traversing unknown worlds with vastly different cultures. In a vast universe where myth and sci-fi intertwine, they constantly encounter surprises and explore the unknown, writing their very own adventure epic. Driven by both brands’ shared passion for technology and exploration, the UGREEN × Honkai: Star Rail collaboration series aims to equip players with their very own highly efficient, on-the-go power gear. Whether working on the move, traveling long distances, teaming up for a gaming session, or keeping multiple devices online, players can ensure every leg of their journey always remains in a fully charged, max HP state.

This collaboration marks a massive level-up for UGREEN within the HoYoverse IP ecosystem. Following their Genshin Impact collaboration, UGREEN is teaming up with Honkai: Star Rail for the very first time to launch a deeply integrated charging series gift box. This also happens to be Yao Guang’s debut in tech crossovers. By perfectly fusing Yao Guang’s knack for “strategic masterminding” and “turning woe into weal” with UGREEN’s high-performance fast-charging system, this collaboration guarantees players an energy replenishment and power management experience truly worthy of a Reignbow Arbiter-General!

The UGREEN MagFlow Air Magnetic Power Bank Honkai: Star Rail Edition pushes the mobile power experience to a whole new dimension. With a sleek 13.9mm profile, it supports 15W magnetic wireless fast charging. Just snap it on to charge! Meanwhile, the built-in Type-C cable supports 30W two-way fast charging, dynamically switching between charging and discharging to achieve a true uninterrupted charge-and-play experience. Furthermore, the device is equipped with Thermal Guard smart temperature control technology and 13-phase safety protection, providing a secure escort for every one of your charges.

The UGREEN Nexode Air Charger Honkai: Star Rail Edition packs a serious punch despite its compact size, supporting up to 65W of high-efficiency GaN fast charging. With its built-in smart power distribution system, it automatically detects your devices and optimizes the power route. Equipped with Thermal Guard smart temperature control and 9-phase safety protection, it’s your most reliable fast-charging companion for any trailblazing journey. The UGREEN Nexode Retractable USB-C Cable Honkai: Star Rail Edition supports up to 100W full-speed fast charging and high-speed data transfer. Its built-in E-marker chip smartly identifies your device’s power requirements to avoid overload. The retractable design greatly improves portability and everyday storage, while the cable itself offers superior tensile strength and long-lasting durability.

The UGREEN FineTrack Mini 2 Honkai: Star Rail Edition brings the smart tracker category into the game’s collaboration lineup for the very first time. Integrated with the Apple Find My ecosystem, it supports real-time device tracking and left-behind alerts, equipped with a 110dB sound ping and location-sharing features to enable comprehensive multi-device management. With IP68 water and dust resistance alongside ultra-long battery life, it ensures reliable use in complex environments.

The UGREEN Nexode Pro Power Bank Honkai: Star Rail Edition is the ultimate energy core for mobile gaming and everyday trailblazing. Packing a 10,000mAh capacity, it supports 45W/55W single-port fast charging, 35W self-charging, and simultaneous output to three devices, keeping your phone, tablet, and earbuds fully powered. Its smart display provides real-time information such as the remaining battery percentage, total input/output power, and fast-charging status. Armed with Thermal Guard smart temperature-control technology and a 14-phase safety-protection mechanism across the board, it ensures continuous, stable output under high loads, perfectly echoing Yao Guang’s stellar dispatch and command capabilities.

* UGREEN Nexode Pro Power Bank Honkai: Star Rail Edition is one of the products in this collaboration series, but it is not included in the collectible gift box. Additionally, the actual products launched and available for sale may vary by region. Please refer to the final local release information.

The UGREEN × Honkai: Star Rail collaboration series is now available for purchase and will gradually roll out to online and offline retail channels worldwide.

Honkai: Star Rail is now available Free-To-Play on PC , iOS , Android , Epic Games Store , and PS5 .

items.title: HoYoverse and UGREEN Are Bringing Honkai: Star Rail to Power Banks, Chargers, and More
items.summary: HoYoverse and UGREEN are bringing Honkai: Star Rail into everyday tech with a new limited-edition accessory line centred on Yao Guang.
items.content:
HoYoverse and UGREEN are bringing Honkai: Star Rail into everyday tech with a new limited-edition accessory line centred on Yao Guang.

HoYoverse and UGREEN are teaming up on a new line of Honkai: Star Rail accessories, bringing the space-fantasy RPG into the world of fast chargers, power banks and travel gear through a limited-edition collection built around the character Yao Guang. The collaboration, promoted under the banner “Power Up for the Next Trailblaze,” is available now and will roll out through online and physical retail channels worldwide, though the companies say product availability may vary by region.

The release is the first collaboration between UGREEN and Honkai: Star Rail , following its earlier work with Genshin Impact , and it looks like both brands are positioning it as a more deeply integrated push that blends fandom appeal with practical hardware for players on the move. In the announcement, the partners said the line is designed to give players “highly efficient, on-the-go power gear,” framing the collection as a way to keep devices charged during travel, work and gaming sessions.

The main focus of the new Honkai: Star Rail collaboration is a lineup of five products: a 13.9 mm magnetic power bank with 15 W wireless charging, a 65 W GaN fast charger, a 10,000 mAh smart-display power bank, a 100 W retractable USB-C cable and a FineTrack Mini 2 tracker that supports Apple’s Find My ecosystem. UGREEN says the hardware leans on features such as Thermal Guard temperature control, multi-layer safety protection and smart power management, while the design language ties each product back to Yao Guang’s in-game identity.

UGREEN seems to be going all out on this new Honkai: Star Rail collaboration, with the brand packaging the collection as a collectible gift box that includes bonus merchandise such as a chibi stand, transparent card, badge, holographic ticket, lanyard and storage pouch. That broader presentation matters because this is as much a lifestyle tie-in as it is a utility play, aimed at fans who want their everyday tech to reflect the games they spend time with.

For UGREEN , the move continues its strategy of pairing mainstream charging accessories with major entertainment brands, while for HoYoverse, it expands Honkai: Star Rail into another consumer category that fits naturally with mobile play and travel, since that is the ideal way to play the game after all. As the companies put it, the goal is to keep every leg of the player journey in a “fully charged, max HP state,” a marketing line, certainly, but one that neatly captures the appeal of this crossover. Check out UGREEN’s website to learn more and to make this new collaboration part of your collection.

This post may contain affiliate links. If you use these links to buy something, CGMagazine may earn a commission. However, please know this does not impact our reviews or opinions in any way. See our ethics statement and review policy .

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail Announced Version 4.4 Special Program
items.summary: Developer HoYoverse officially announced Honkai: Star Rail - Version 4.4 special program. Mark your calendars: the livestream will be kicking off on Friday, July 3rd, at 19:30 (UTC+8). You can catch it on the official
items.content:
Developer HoYoverse officially announced Honkai: Star Rail – Version 4.4 special program. Mark your calendars: the livestream will be kicking off on Friday, July 3rd, at 19:30 (UTC+8). You can catch it on the official YouTube or Twitch accounts.

The special program will introduce Himeko • Nova , as well as details on the collaboration between Honkai: Star Rail and Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works]! Prepare for the arrival of two more Fate mainstays: Rin Tohsaka and Gilgamesh . The developers will also be on hand to give the first glimpse into the new story, events, and share the promocodes for some extra Jades, as per tradition.

“The Navigator charts a course to the apocalypse. A mournful whistle howls, and only those of the Trailblaze can find a path to survival across the realm of death… / Oh? Is that so.”

items.title: The Future of Honkai Star Rail and Fate/stay night Collaboration to be Revealed on July 3rd - Update 4.4 Presentation Teaser
items.content:
0 Источник: miHoYo (HoYoverse) 29 Jun 13:56 The creators of Honkai Star Rail are preparing to host a presentation for the upcoming update 4.3, titled "Whistle at the Edge of the World". Such events by miHoYo (HoYoverse) are accompanied by the release of promo codes for free gifts.

The Honkai Star Rail 4.4 livestream is scheduled for July 3rd (Friday), 2:30 PM Moscow time:

"The Navigator charts a course to the end of the world. A mournful whistle sounds, and only the Trailblazers can find the path of life through the boundaries of death..." - Oh, I see...

The developers of Honkai Star Rail have published a brief announcement for the special livestream — miHoYo plans to talk about the new character Himeko: Nova, and also share information about the HSR x Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] collaboration.

The developers also stated that the version 4.5 character preview section will be published on the morning of June 30th.

Previously, the creators of Honkai Star Rail had already shown new heroes from Fate/stay night [Unlimited Blade Works] — Gilgamesh will be available, as well as Rin Tohsaka, both characters will have 5-star rarity.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.3 guide: Events, free Jades, & banner schedule
Hands on with the Honkai: Star Rail v4.0 experience
items.summary: This HoyoVerse train just keeps on chugging!
items.content:
The last article I wrote for Honkai: Star Rail was going over the details of the upcoming Version 4.0 release . Since then, HoYoverse generously offered up the opportunity to play Version 4.0 and experience Planarcadia early. Having spent about a dozen hours or so playing with the new characters, experiencing the story, and generally taking in the sights and sounds of Planarcadia, I’m prepared to share my thoughts on all of it.

It’s best to start off with the main attraction of Version 4.0: Planarcadia. This new planet is a fictional world come to life and boy does it leave a fantastic first impression. Full of the kind of sights and sounds you’d expect from an Asian city brimming with life. Exploration is a blast with all the alleys and little nooks to find, the platforming segments that can either help you navigate across certain parts of the city or find chests within them. There’s also a new style of puzzles that require you to rotate a 2D plane in a 3D space to “paint” certain parts of it to make them solid and let you platform with those objects. Planarcadia is so full of whimsy and still has some of the overhang of a crime family really enabling a super unique feeling planet compared to what we’ve had with previous locations such as the Xianzhou or Amphoreus.

The two new characters following the path of Elation feel great, even if the Aha Moment is a little overstimulating sometimes. Sparxie and Yao Guang fit on a team together like a glove to demolish all the bosses I’ve thrown them against. I’m eager to see where the new path goes with time, especially since we only have the two characters currently. Even as of the live public server branch, we’re unsure when Elation Trailblazer will make their debut, so keep an eye on that as versions progress beyond 4.0. You’ll need to get a hold of new Elation Light Cones as well since they obviously can’t use the other Paths’ options. Burning skill points to increase the Elation stacks until Aha decides he’s satisfied and steps in for a big attack feels really great and it has me curious what future Elation characters might do when they trigger their own Aha Moments.

Not to mention that for new players, you’ve got the opportunity to really kickstart a team of your choice with a new planet. We’re at a point in the game where there’s so many options for characters you can pick from and build around, and 4.0 introducing Elation means that as a new player, you can jump in and get a headstart on pulling for that archetype. Especially with a new Novaflare round of buffs, there’s even more options than before bringing older characters back into the limelight. The Black Swan and Sparkle buffs are so massive that people running DoT teams or any team using an action advance support such as Bronya or Sunday have another insane option. Sparkle was always good, but her Novaflare enables her to further buff your DPS units to extreme levels. Black Swan’s Novaflare was even more insane and rocketed her back up to the top for damage even as a DoT unit. Combined with the option to pick a free character including units like Sparkle, Kafka (who works great in DoT teams), and more, you really can’t go wrong in Planarcadia and the Version 4.0 patch from what I’ve gotten to experience.

Between the free characters, the great change of pace in the story from the latter half of Amphoreus (if you’ve played it, you know), the new cityscape that is quite honestly my new favorite location in the game, and all the new characters to look forward to, you really should make a return to Star Rail if you’ve been away for a minute and are curious about where we’re at. If you’re new, definitely consider giving it a shot. There’s even a bonus to let you convert trace materials for leveling character skills at a 1 to 1 ratio this time instead of a 2 for 1, so you really can kickstart your Elation team. With characters being important again like Silver Wolf, Blade, and Himeko, as well as new faces like Sparxie, Yao Guang, Ashveil, and Pearl, I think 4.0 is just the start of a real treat of a patch cycle for Honkai: Star Rail.

items.title: Giving a game another chance
items.summary: Honkai: Star Rail (HSR) celebrated its three-year anniversary on April 26, 2026. I originally started playing back when it first came out, but I stopped not too long after because I didn’t really like it. That was because I had just been playing a different game from HoYoverse, the company that makes HSR, that I...
items.content:
Honkai: Star Rail (HSR) celebrated its three-year anniversary on April 26, 2026. I originally started playing back when it first came out, but I stopped not too long after because I didn’t really like it.

That was because I had just been playing a different game from HoYoverse, the company that makes HSR, that I had to stop playing because of bugs. I also stopped because it was really laggy, but I realized when I started playing again this past December that I had actually originally played on the European server rather than the American one, which was why it was lagging.

Now that I’ve started playing it again for the last four months, I’m wishing I’d never quit.

The game’s story is amazing. With games like HSR where the main character is a self-insert, I tend to fully lean into that role. As such, whenever a major twist happens to a character that the main character has bonded with, I really feel it due to the immersion.

Another thing I like about the story is that HSR keeps you on your toes because it isn’t afraid to kill off playable characters.

I’d played a different game from HoYoverse before HSR, and it wouldn’t dream of killing a playable character, so I went into HSR thinking it’d be the same. That idea went out the window on my second day of playing with the first of many playable character deaths.

Because of that, you end up wanting to make sure you own your favorite characters in case you never see them in the game again. However, since HSR is a gacha game, you’re not guaranteed to get the characters you want when they’re available without spending money.

At least that’s what you’d think, but from my experience, HSR is the easiest gacha game I’ve played when it comes to getting the characters you want without spending money.

I’ve managed to get three characters I’ve wanted along with two of their exclusive items without spending money. That’s because HSR makes it really easy to get the in-game currency for pulling characters. That means if you just want to get a character for the sake of having them, you can.

In fact, I’d say HSR is probably the only gacha game where you don’t have to spend real money. I only spent money on cosmetics, but most cosmetics are free rewards from permanent events.

The only negative thing I have to say about the game is that the newer main quests are fairly long.

The latest finished questline takes 62 hours to complete, and the entire main quest line is currently over 100 hours. Because of that, if you have a busy daily schedule, it could take a while to complete. It is possible to finish in a week, but based on personal experience, I wouldn’t recommend it.

The length aside, I would highly recommend this game, especially since it’s free on PC and you don’t even need a gaming one as I play on my laptop. With the three years of content in the game so far, I can’t wait to see where it goes from here.

items.title: Honkai: Star Rail 4.0 impressions
items.content:
I'll admit, I recently burned out on Honkai: Star Rail. Upon hearing the news that 3.8 would be the lengthiest patch yet and with content more sparse than ever before, I decided to take a break for the first time since I started playing all the way back in 1.0. The entire Amphoreus saga left me feeling drained; I thoroughly enjoyed 3.0-3.4 as excellent set-up chapters, but felt lost as the plot became a labyrinth of hidden motivations, layered twists, hours of exposition, and endless proper nouns. When looking at the story as a whole, I love the characters (Aglaea and Anaxa in particular) and the rich history of the world within the Scepter, but the later patches often felt like studying for a needlessly convoluted test. I understood the gist of what was happening, but I won't lie to you and say I could describe to you in detail what Cyrene's deal is or how Evernight fits into everything.

All that's to say: I was looking forward to the Astral Express crew taking a well-earned vacation to Planarcadia, the planet of Elation. Despite two of the game's four current versions ostensibly beginning with Akivili's disciples deciding that they need a break from the backbreaking work of saving the universe, neither has resulted in our trailblazers seeing much downtime. Unlike previous excursions, it's all hands on deck: everyone gets off at this stop (save for Black Swan's incredibly hasty and unfortunate exit prior to departure). It's fun to see the entire main cast get involved, none more welcome than Himeko — up until now, the only character trait I could've told you about her was that she's terrible at making coffee. Getting some elaboration on her backstory and giving her a personality outside of "Express mom" is long overdue.

The Trailblazer and March 7th begin their Planarcadian getaway with a suitably devious greeting by none other than our old pal Sparkle. We're given a tour of the vibrant, totally-not-Japanese-inspired streets, crawling with humans living in tentative harmony with totally-not-Penaconian-memetic-entities known as imagenae. Here, we catch a glimpse of what I hope will become one of Planarcadia's core themes: gentrification and the plight of capitalism, with a side of anti-imperialism (all perpetrated by the IPC, of course). It's bold, though, and I worry that future patches will keep these criticisms in the background rather than highlight them as Planarcadia's core issues. I would love so much for little nuggets of lore to be elaborated upon, ones that suggest a more serious political history than Aha would have you believe: what did the imagenae do before the IPC forced them to integrate into society? Were they not allowed to work at all? Were they marginalized and seen as second-class citizens? Is the Department of Aberration Defense an inherently evil organization left to determine on their own which imagenae deserve their continued existence and which do not? Is Kuchiba so hot that it doesn't even matter?

This brings us to the Synwish Syndicate, a gang formerly designed to be a respite for imagenae that feel rejected elsewhere, now, due to IPC intervention, a gang that largely exists to enable monetary gain by questionable means. Here at the syndicate's headquarters, the plot quickly begins to balloon into what feels like a collection of introductions for what's to come. We have Pearl's cold and calculating grip on the planet, Himeko and her confounding family history, the potential return of the Herald of Death, the Stellaron Hunters' unknown involvement in the Phantasmoon Games, Sunday's inadvertent entry into the Games, Evanescia's whole...thing, and Yao Guang's bottomless supply of divinations. What's most shocking is that the patch still manages to pull off a fun (if bloated) self-contained story, adding layers to one of HSR's most inscrutable characters and ending things with one of the game's best low-stakes twists.

I'm no Xianzhou Arbiter-General, but I can pretty easily peer into the future and divine a prophecy for Honkai: Star Rail: each new patch will introduce and/or discuss at length a new prophecy that cryptically divulges the fate of a character and/or planet and/or simulation masquerading as a planet. It's hard to pinpoint the moment a theme becomes a crutch, but I think HSR passed that moment long ago. In this patch, we have not one, not two, but three central characters that are defined by their relationship with prophecy and prediction — Yao Guang, Pearl, and the perennial mystery that is Elio and his script. So many in-game conversations devolve into the interpretation of prophecy, and frankly, it's becoming exhausting. We went through an entire version in which each Chrysos Heir had a poetically vague predetermined fate, and before that (and in 3.8's ret-con-y confusion) we had Sparkle's ambiguous "three deaths" for Firefly.

If this were a wholesale replacement for well-executed foreshadowing, we would have a far bigger issue. It's obvious, though, that the writing team knows how to tell a story sans-prophecy, evident in Sparkle's character-driven narrative in this very patch. Sparxie being the "true" Sparkle (as much as old Sparkle is also the "true" Sparkle) is teased so perfectly; the reveal is obvious in retrospect while being difficult to predict with the given information. It's consistently groan-inducing to see this writing cheat code being used ad nauseam within a story that could function better without it. I hope I'll be proven wrong and the game will subvert the words of its murky prophets, leaving me with egg on my face come the conclusion of this leg of the Express' journey.

4.0 is a promising new beginning, and has at the very least brought me back in terms of its story. Though there is an abundance of new proper nouns to memorize and a bevy of new and returning cast members to keep track of, the patch still takes on a welcome return to simplicity and levity (save for the shocking tonal shift right at the end, RIP the Synwish Syndicate Feb. 2026 - Feb. 2026). As long as things don't get too bogged down in extensive metaphors and tiresome exposition, I'm hopeful that Planarcadia can become the most focused version of HSR we've seen so far.
